title: Refractive index adjusted imaging medium: High RI Iodixanol, Iohexol - Yeast
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1sth7","text":"This protocol describes the steps to prepare imaging medium for Saccharomyces cerevisiae with high refractive index. This medium is optimized for fluorescence imaging by the reduction of auto-fluorescence through an abundance of Adenine [1] and the repression of of the Met-promoted pp7- CP expression [2].","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Compound medium for autoclave
The medium can be store at the bench for 2 to 3 months.
When the medium cooled down to around 80 °C add 3 mL sterile Glucose 20%.
Autoclave for 00:15:00 at 121 °C . Remove the stirring bar before going to autoclave.

Add and resolve Iodixanol (~Optiprep) crystals. For reasons of cost efficiency it possible to use a portion of the mixed solution with 1.4 and add the Iodixanol crystals to increase the RI. ! Be aware that the Glucose 20% volume added in step 3 must be adjusted to the correct ratio for the volume used!
Add 1.4 mL ddH 2 O.
Add 201.6 mg Yeast Nitrogen Base with Ammonium Sulfate without Amino Acids (YNB). Warming the medium on the stirring plate helps resolving the powders.

Add 57.6 mg SC-XX powder. In regard to cover all optional dropout media the amino acid base holds the notification -xx, where xx stand for the amino acid(s) that is as selection factor, missing in the medium.
Add 0.3 mL Methionine 200x solution. The additional Methionine represses the Met promoter, which drives PP7 syntheses [2].
Add 0.3 mL Adenine 100x solution. The additional Adenine is supposed to repress the Adenine synthesize to reduce a possible accumulation of red pigment [1].

It is possible to use existing medium with Iodexanol adjusted refractive index as a base. In this case only steps 1.7 and 1.8 apply. Fill a 50 ml flask with 25 mL OptiPrep. Add a magnetic stirring bar and place the flask on a stirring hot plate.
The medium can be store at the bench for 2 to 3 months.
When the medium cooled down to around 80 °C add 3 mL sterile Glucose 20%.
Autoclave for 00:15:00 at 121 °C . Remove the stirring bar before going to autoclave.

Add Iohexol (Histodenz) powder. For reasons of cost efficiency it possible to use a portion of the mixed solution to add the Iohexol for the RI adjustment. ! Be aware that the Glucose 20% volume added in step 3 must be adjusted to the correct ratio for refractive index adjusted portion used! %s %s Warming the medium on the stirring plate helps resolving the powders.
Add 1.5 mL YNB 20x solution (Yeast Nitrogen Base with Ammonium Sulfate without Amino Acids).

Add 3 mL SC-XX 10x solution. In regard to cover all optional dropout media the amino acid base holds the notification -xx, where xx stand for the amino acid(s) that is as selection factor, missing in the medium.
Add 0.3 mL Methionine 200x solution. The additional Methionine represses the Met promoter, which drives PP7 syntheses [2].
Add 0.3 mL Adenine 100x solution. The additional Adenine is supposed to repress the Adenine synthesize to reduce a possible accumulation of red pigment [1].
Fill a 50 ml flask with 21.9 mL ddH 2 O Add a magnetic stirring bar and place the flask on a stirring hot plate.
Measure the refractive index with a refractometer. If the RI measured is below your target RI, repeat step 1.7 go to step #1.7 until the targeted RI is reached.
creator: Mathias Hammer
